HTML is the default [[Using text components|text component]] in SuperMemo. It is based on editing and rendering engines of Internet Explorer. As such it carries all the rendering power and compatibility range of this powerful Microsoft product. This also means that all bugs and shortcomings should also be familiar. The good news is that, in Windows 10, most of the old HTLM problems are gone.

As all necessary Internet Explorer components are part of Windows, you do not need to worry about installing Internet Explorer. '''You can use any web browser while using SuperMemo'''.

There are 3 ways in which you are most likely to add HTML text to your [[Glossary:Collection|collection]] ('''Middle''' or higher [[Glossary:Level|level]]):

# '''Notes''': if you would like to type in some notes that will later be processed with [[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]], use '''[[Edit menu|Edit]] : [[Edit menu#Add a note|Add a note]]''' (''Alt+N''). For example, after hearing a piece of science news on TV, you may want to quickly type in a note to SuperMemo. Press ''Alt+N'' and type in the message.
# '''Articles''': if you find an interesting article on the net, select it in your web browser and copy it to the clipboard (e.g. with ''Ctrl+C''). You can then paste the article stored in the clipboard to SuperMemo with ''Ctrl+N'' ('''[[Edit menu|Edit]] : [[Edit menu#Add a new article|Add a new article]]'''). '''Article''' is similar to '''Note'''. The difference is that '''Article''' will paste the text from the clipboard to your newly created [[Glossary:Element|element]], whereas '''Note''' will create a ''blank'' new element.
# '''Multiple articles''': if you have a couple of articles opened in a [[web browser]], use '''[[Edit menu|Edit]] : [[Web import]]''' (''Shift+F8''). For more details, see [[Web import]].

<div class="center">

[[Image:Incremental_reading_Mnemonic_cues2.jpg|800px|SuperMemo: A topic about henipavirus in the HTML component on the left and 4 images taken from it with Download images (Ctrl+F8) in their individual image components on the right]]

<blockquote>

'''''Figure:''' A [[Glossary:Topic|topic]] with an article about <span style="color: maroon;">henipavirus</span> in the HTML component on the left and 4 images taken from the article with '''[[Component menu#Download images|Download images]]''' (Ctrl+F8) (available on the [[Component menu|component context menu]]), which downloaded, localized, and tiled them in their individual image [[Glossary:Component|components]] on the right. These 4 images will propagate to all [[Glossary:Extract|extracts]] and [[Glossary:Cloze_deletion|cloze deletions]] ultimately produced from this article.''

</blockquote>

</div>

If you want to import an HTML file to an existing HTML component, right-click the HTML component, and choose '''[[Component menu#File|File]] : [[Component menu#Import file|Import file]]'''. Pick the file in the dialog box and choose '''OK'''. Your HTML file will be displayed in the HTML component. '''[[Component menu#Import file|Import file]]''' does not import files linked to by the imported file. However, you can import an entire website located on your hard disk or local network if you use '''[[Component menu#Links|Links]] : External file''' on the HTML [[component menu]]. In such a case, the HTML file will remain in its original location and retain all relative links.

If you would like to edit an HTML text in your favorite HTML editor, press ''Ctrl+F9'' (or choose '''[[Component menu#File|File]] : Edit file''' from the HTML [[component menu]]) and your HTML code will open in the HTML editor registered for editing HTML files in Windows. To preview an article in your web browser, ''Ctrl+click'' the HTML component or press ''F9'' (or choose '''[[Component menu#File|File]] : View file''' from the HTML [[component menu]]).

== Inserting hyperlinks ==

The most useful hyperlink will connect keywords with other [[Glossary:Element|elements]] in SuperMemo. To create inter-element hyperlink in HTML:
# display the target element in SuperMemo
# copy its number to the clipboard (e.g. by using ''Ctrl+G'')
# select the linking keyword in the linking element
# paste the element number with ''Shift+Ins'' (note that ''Ctrl+V'' would paste the actual element number)

To insert hyperlinks that would point to other [[Glossary:Element|elements]], registry texts, HTML files, URL addresses on the Internet, registry images, etc., read about the option [[HTML link menu|'''Insert HTML link''']] (HTML [[component menu]]).

The HTML component can interpret JavaScript. If you know some JavaScript commands, you can enhance your SuperMemo presentation with scripted HTML components.

== See also ==

* [https://super-memory.com/help/faq/html.htm FAQ: Using HTML in SuperMemo]
* [[Using text components|Text components]]
* [https://super-memory.com/help/faq/texts.htm FAQ: Text components]
* [https://supermemopedia.com/wiki/Only_one_bullet_is_created_for_the_entire_paragraph Formatting bullets]
